title: corymbia citriodora subsp. variegata
intro: Corymbia citriodora subsp. variegata, also known as Lemon-Scented Gum, is a subspecies of Corymbia citriodora, a flowering plant native to Australia. It is valued for its essential oil, which contains citronellal and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Corymbia citriodora subsp. variegata include its use in treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. The essential oil can be obtained through steam distillation and is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
